entry.title: From NUL to DEL: Why 7 Bit ASCII IS Actually Really Clever
entry.quote: For the vast majority of folks working in tech these days, ASCII is just part of the fabric of technology: the idea that anybody actually designed it might come as a bit of a shock. But they did, and I think they actually did a really good job. Let's take a wander through the 7-bit ASCII character set and meet some of my favourite bits.

entry.title: Colossal Chronography: Frances Harriet Lightfoot’s 1831 Marvel
entry.quote: One reason to envy long-past information designers is the big canvases they got to play with. Consider this 1831 timeline next to my iPhone—it’s big, isn’t it?

entry.title: Google is a monopoly. The fix isn't obvious
entry.quote: A business breakup may be coming – but what comes after may not be better

entry.title: Process Zero: The Anti-Intelligent Camera
entry.quote: Introducing a process that uses zero AI and zero computational photography to produce beautiful, film-like natural photos. Meet Halide 2.15, with Process Zero.
